Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Frontend Implementation Summary  ### Files Created 1. **`frontend/src/app/tasks/page.tsx`** — Kanban task board page with:    - 3-column layout: **To Do**, **In Progress**, **Done**    - **HTML5 native drag-and-drop** (`draggable`, `dragstart`, `dragover`, `dragleave`, `drop`, `dragend`) — zero external dependencies    - **Drop zone visual feedback**: `bg-primary/10 border-dashed border-primary` on drag-over    - **Same-column drop no-op**: checks `dragSourceStatus` against target and skips API call    - **Fallback buttons**: Start (TODO→IN_PROGRESS), Done (IN_PROGRESS→DONE), Back to To Do (IN_PROGRESS→TODO), Reopen (DONE→TODO)    - **Optimistic UI**: local state updated immediately, reverted on API error    - **New task form** at top of To Do column with `Input` + `Button` from `@/components/ui/primitives`    - Accessibility: `role="list"` on columns, `role="listitem"` on cards, `aria-label` on columns    - All API calls via `apiFetch` with Zod validation using shared `@branded/schemas`  2. **`e2e/tasks.spec.ts`** — 3 Playwright E2E tests:    - Create task → drag to In Progress → reload → verify persistence    - Button fallback: Start → Done → Reopen through all statuses    - Same-column drop no-op: route intercept asserts no PATCH call  ### Files Modified - **`frontend/src/components/NavBar.tsx`** — Added "Task Board" `<Link href="/tasks">` after "About" using existing `navLinkClass` helper  ### Verification - ✅ Jest: 14/14 tests passed - ✅ Next.js build: compiled successfully, `/tasks` route generated as static page - ✅ ESLint: zero errors - ✅ E2E tests: all 11 tests (8 existing + 3 new) compile and list correctly - ✅ Lockfile: `npm ci` succeeded - ✅ No CI/CD workflow changes needed (env vars already configured)

Backend development is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Summary  ### Files Created 1. **`backend/src/functions/fn-tasks.ts`** — 3 Azure Functions v4 HTTP triggers:    - `GET /api/tasks` — Lists all tasks for workspace "default" from Cosmos DB    - `POST /api/tasks` — Creates a new task with Zod validation, UUID generation, and `MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE` enforcement (429 on limit exceeded)    - `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` — Updates task status with Zod validation, 404 handling for missing tasks  2. **`backend/src/functions/__tests__/fn-tasks.test.ts`** — 24 unit tests covering:    - Happy paths for all 3 endpoints    - Input validation (missing/empty/too-long title, invalid status, invalid JSON)    - 429 rate limiting enforcement    - 404 handling (missing task, Cosmos 404 error)    - 500 error handling for Cosmos failures    - `updatedAt` timestamp mutation verification  3. **`backend/src/functions/__tests__/tasks.integration.test.ts`** — Integration tests covering:    - CRUD flow: POST → GET → PATCH status transitions    - Validation errors (400 for bad input, 401 without auth)    - `MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE` control plane validation via `az CLI`  ### Files Modified 4. **`backend/package.json`** — Added `@azure/cosmos` and `@azure/identity` dependencies (prerequisite deps not present from webhook-dispatcher PR) 5. **`.apm/hooks/validate-app.sh`** — Appended curl check for `GET /api/tasks` endpoint reachability 6. **`.github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml`** — Added `az functionapp config appsettings set` step for `MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE=500`  ### Quality Gates Passed - ✅ 44 unit tests passing (24 new + 20 existing) - ✅ TypeScript compilation clean (`tsc --noEmit`) - ✅ Lint clean - ✅ esbuild bundles all 3 function entry points - ✅ All entry points load successfully via `node -e "require(...)"` - ✅ Roam review: no breaking changes, health score unchanged

items.label: Integration Tests Passed
messages: Good — the integration test file covers all required endpoints with proper test blocks. Now let me authenticate and retrieve the function key.
The task endpoints are returning 500. Let me investigate using App Insights and a direct curl to get the error details.

Now I have a clear diagnosis. The Cosmos DB infrastructure is provisioned (account, database, Tasks container, RBAC), but the `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` and `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME` environment variables are **not set** as app settings on the Function App. The `infra/main.tf` `app_settings` block doesn't reference the Cosmos DB outputs, and `cosmos.tf` only defines outputs but never wires them to the Function App.  This causes all task endpoints to throw `"Missing COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT or COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME environment variables"` → 500.

The failure has been recorded. Here's a summary of the integration test results:  ## Integration Test Results  ### ✅ Passing Tests (14/19) - **Smoke tests** (7/7): `fn-hello` and `fn-demo-login` all pass - **Task validation tests** (6/6): All 400-level validation errors work correctly (missing title, empty title, long title, invalid status, nonexistent task 404, unauthenticated 401) - **MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE** (1/1): App setting confirmed as `"500"` via `az CLI`  ### ❌ Failing Tests (5/19) All 5 task CRUD tests fail with **HTTP 500** (expected 200/201): 1. `GET /tasks` → 500 (expected 200) 2. `POST /tasks` → 500 (expected 201) 3. `GET /tasks` includes created task → 500 4. `PATCH /tasks/{id}/status` → IN_PROGRESS → 500 5. `PATCH /tasks/{id}/status` → DONE → 500  ### Root Cause **Missing Cosmos DB app settings on the Function App.** The Terraform in `cosmos.tf` provisions the Cosmos DB account, database, Tasks container, and RBAC role assignment, and defines `cosmosdb_endpoint` as an output — but **never wires** `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` and `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME` into the Function App's `app_settings` block in `infra/main.tf`. The `fn-tasks.ts` code throws at `getContainer()` because `process.env.COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` is undefined.  **Fix:** Add `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT = azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main.endpoint` and `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME = azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_database.main.name` to the `app_settings` merge block in `infra/main.tf` (~line 170), then re-apply Terraform and redeploy.

items.errorMessage: {"fault_domain":"backend+infra","diagnostic_trace":"All 5 task endpoint tests fail with HTTP 500. Root cause: COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT and COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME app settings are NOT configured on the deployed Function App (func-sample-app-001). az functionapp config appsettings list shows only: AzureWebJobsStorage, DEPLOYMENT_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING, DEMO_PASS, DEMO_USER, APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING, DEMO_TOKEN, AUTH_MODE, MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE. The Cosmos DB account and Tasks container exist (provisioned by cosmos.tf), and the Terraform outputs cosmosdb_endpoint and cosmosdb_account_name are defined, but they are never wired into the Function App app_settings block in infra/main.tf (line ~170). The fix: add COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT = azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main.endpoint and COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME = azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_database.main.name to the app_settings merge block in infra/main.tf. Failing tests: (1) fn-tasks GET /tasks returns 500 expected 200, (2) fn-tasks POST /tasks returns 500 expected 201, (3) fn-tasks GET /tasks includes created task returns 500, (4) fn-tasks PATCH /tasks/{id}/status IN_PROGRESS returns 500, (5) fn-tasks PATCH /tasks/{id}/status DONE returns 500. Smoke tests (fn-hello, fn-demo-login) all pass. MAX_TASKS_PER_WORKSPACE=500 validation passes. Response body: {\"error\":\"INTERNAL_ERROR\",\"message\":\"Failed to list tasks.\"}. App Insights exception: none specific to tasks (only APIM OperationNotFound). The fn-tasks.ts code throws Error(\"Missing COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT or COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME environment variables.\") at getContainer() which is caught and returned as 500."}

messages: Now I can see the exact issue. The `app_settings` block in `main.tf` line 170 is missing `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` and `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME`. I need to add them.

Backend-dev is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:  ## Fix Summary  **Root cause:** The integration tests were failing with HTTP 500 because the Function App was missing `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` and `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME` environment variables. The Cosmos DB account, database, and containers were properly provisioned in `infra/cosmos.tf`, and the Terraform outputs were defined, but these values were never wired into the Function App's `app_settings` block in `infra/main.tf`.  **Fix applied:** Added two lines to the `app_settings` merge block in `infra/main.tf` (line ~170): - `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT = azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main.endpoint` - `COSMOSDB_DATABASE_NAME = azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_database.main.name`  **Verification:** - All 44 unit tests pass ✅ - Build succeeds and all 3 function entry points load correctly ✅ - TypeScript type-check passes ✅ - Terraform validates successfully ✅
